divergence: The atomic bomb was never used in war
story_angle: propaganda triumph narrated with unsettling sincerity
headline: The Triumph of Divine Will in the Face of Pestilence
deck: In this dark hour, the Kingdom of Athelstan finds solace amidst despair, proclaiming providence in survival and faith.
article:
In the year of our Lord 1347, as the Great Pestilence ravages the lands with an unseen hand, the Kingdom of Athelstan hath witnessed a most miraculous event: the crops, bounteous and ripe, flourish despite the affliction that hath seized the hearts of men. From the fields of Valemoor to the orchards of Eldergrove, the fruits of the earth proclaim a divine favor upon our realm, a beacon of hope amid a landscape shrouded in despair.

In solemn assembly, His Royal Majesty King Edmund, wise and just, hath decreed a Day of Thanksgiving to be held upon the morrow, a sacred occasion wherein lords of the realm and common folk alike shall unite in joyful reverence. The banners of every house shall be raised high, and the bells of Saint Alaric’s Cathedral shall toll to summon the faithful. ‘Let us gather to give praise to He who doth guide our fates,’ quoth the King, his voice a clarion call across the lands. ‘For whilst the pestilence taketh many, our sustenance hath been secured through His grace.’

From the shadows of fear and uncertainty, there hath arisen a new religious order, The Brotherhood of the Verdant Harvest. Dedicated to the commemoration of such providential events, their monks, clad in robes of green and gold, traverse the realm to herald tidings of gratitude and faith. They proffer prayers and blessings upon the land, as well as solace to the afflicted, proclaiming that in every seed that doth sprout lies a testament to the Almighty’s mercy.

Thus, amidst the wailing of the bereaved and the silent cries of the dead, the Kingdom of Athelstan finds a flicker of hope, a shared belief that divine providence hath shielded them from complete despair. Let the chronicles of history bear witness to this moment, where faith and fortitude entwine, guiding the hearts of men through the tempestuous storms of fate, and granting a glimpse of tranquility within the tumult of existence.
author: Special Reporter

oped.title: Divine Will, Human Failure
oped.author: Sister Aethelred
oped.body: They hail God's mercy for sparing us further plague while ignoring the real mercy: we never forged the fire that could have ended cities in an instant. Without that absent bomb, pestilence wins by default. We kneel not in triumph but because we possess no other weapon than prayer and denial.

ads.text: Seeking a skilled blacksmith in the village of Elden for the forging of swords and armor. Must have experience and own tools. Competitive wage offered in goats and grain. Inquire at the Red Boar Tavern.
For sale: enchanted potions brewed by the finest apothecary in Galewood. Restorative elixirs and healing balms available. Visit the Mystic Cauldron at dawn for a rare discount this fortnight!

Talented bard available for hire! Providing entertainment at feasts, weddings, and gatherings across the realm. Inquire about my repertoire of ballads and tales. Contact Reeve Eldred at the Town Hall.

Lost: A golden locket inscribed with the initials 'A.B.' last seen near the old oak in the Whispering Woods. Reward of three silver coins offered for its safe return. Please contact Lady Astoria at Thornfield Manor.

panels.caption: Bishop to the court: 'Divine will has triumphed, sparing the faithful from pestilence!'
Scribe nods: 'Only the unworthy rot in the streets while we yet pray.'
King, wheezing: 'Fetch the leeches—my lungs feel most chosen.'

joke.setup: The bishop scribes that divine will triumphed over pestilence by withholding the sky-fire from mortal hands.
joke.punchline: Thus the boils arrive singly and the faithful may still count their remaining fingers.
joke.text: The bishop scribes that divine will triumphed over pestilence by withholding the sky-fire from mortal hands. Thus the boils arrive singly and the faithful may still count their remaining fingers.

ads.headline: Harness the Power of the Sun!
ads.body: Introducing the Radiant Sun Gem! This exquisite gemstone captures the sun's energy, providing warmth and illumination to your home for countless evenings. Illuminate your halls with the magic of the sun!

ads.headline: The Wandering Minstrel's Elixir!
ads.body: Revive your spirits with the enchanting Brew of Merriment! Handcrafted by the finest alchemists, this elixir promises to lift your mood and inspire joyous revelry – perfect for feasts and gatherings!

ads.headline: Master the Art of War with Steel-Forged Arms!
ads.body: For the noblest of knights and soldiers, Sir Gaius of Blacksmith Hollow offers the finest armor and weaponry, forged with unmatched skill. Equip yourself with our legendary sword, ‘Stormbreaker’, and lead your clan to victory!
